title: PHP String Formatting
content:
### PHP String Formatting

PHP provides several ways to format strings with dynamic values.

### printf / sprintf

`printf()` outputs a formatted string. `sprintf()` returns it.

```php
<?php
printf("Hello, %s! You are %d years old.", "John", 25);
// Hello, John! You are 25 years old.

$formatted = sprintf("Price: $%.2f", 19.956);
echo $formatted; // Price: $19.96
?>
```

### Format Specifiers

| Specifier | Type | Example |
|-----------|------|---------|
| `%s` | String | `"hello"` |
| `%d` | Integer (decimal) | `42` |
| `%f` | Float | `3.14` |
| `%b` | Binary | `1010` |
| `%x` | Hex (lowercase) | `ff` |
| `%X` | Hex (uppercase) | `FF` |
| `%%` | Literal % | `%` |

### Padding and Precision

```php
<?php
// Padding
printf("'%10s'\n", "right");   // '     right'
printf("'%-10s'\n", "left");    // 'left      '
printf("'%'*10s'\n", "stars");  // '*****stars'

// Precision
printf("%.2f\n", 3.14159);      // 3.14
printf("%.5s\n", "Hello World"); // Hello
?>
```

### Number Formatting

```php
<?php
echo number_format(1234567.89, 2, '.', ',');
// 1,234,567.89

echo number_format(1234567.89, 2, ',', ' ');
// 1 234 567,89 (European style)
?>
```

> **Key Point:** Use `sprintf()` when you need to store the formatted string in a variable instead of printing it directly.

"; echo number_format(1234567.891, 3, '.', ''); ?>

What is the difference between printf and sprintf?
